text:
TheParis Olympicsgot underway at the weekend afterthe opening ceremony had come in for criticism, with athletes sailing on boats down the River Seine. And the medals have come thick and fast, with a whole host of competitions already done and dusted.

Team USA will be aiming to finish at the top of themedal table, with China and France also competing for the same honour.

But there is already confusion over the table, with Americans bragging they’re top, despite trailing other countries in the gold medal count.

The International Olympic Committee, along with virtually every country, ranks nations based on the total number of gold medals they have won, followed by silver and bronze medals. But the USA break the usual convention by ranking nations based on the overall number of medals they have won.

As of Thursday afternoon, China were at the top of the official medal table after clinching 11 golds, with France in second with eight. Japan also had eight gold medals to their name but were behind the French team as they had seven fewer silvers.

Australia were in fourth with seven golds, while the USA, Great Britain and South Korea had all accumulated six.

Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

While the USA were way down in fifth place overall, they were positioned well ahead of the chasing pack in their own ranking system as they had picked up more medals than any other nation in total.

According to the USA medal table, the North American country were leading the way with 31 medals, with France in second on 26. And China were in third with 21 medals, one more than Great Britain had managed.

The USA’s first six golds came in the men’s 4 x 100m freestyle swimming, the women's 100m butterfly, the women’s individual foil fencing,the women’s team gymnastics, the women’s 1500m freestyle swimming and the men’s four rowing.

And some followers of the Olympics on X have lashed out for the way the USA represent the medal table.

One fan, @Tommytugboat404, wrote: “Very impressive medal table, which will look totally different in the USA. Love how they base it on medals won, just to keep them on top.”

Meanwhile, @theposhfan penned: “Always makes me laugh that US publications change the #Olympics medal table to put themselves on top, whereas the rest of the world show the universally accepted table and just accept they may not be number one.”

And @JoelSeidl added: “Only America uses this table and solely to make them look better. The real table is the Olympic medal table. Everybody uses it except America. It’s really pathetic tbh, you make yourself a fool by using this.”

title: Olympic medal table confusion has Americans bragging they're top of the tree | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Tom Parsons
description: Team USA are aiming to finish at the top of the medal table at the Paris Olympics and already think they're leading the way.
